post.description:
Create a new creator (artist) profile.

Validates that the canonical name is unique to prevent duplicates.

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of all creators.

Supports text search against canonical names and filtering by creator type (e.g., photographer, illustrator).

post.description:
Merge multiple duplicate creators into a single primary creator.

All sets associated with the source IDs will be reassigned to the target ID. The source creator records will then be permanently deleted.

Merge multiple source sets into a single target set.

All images from the source sets will be reassigned to the target set. The source sets will then be permanently deleted. Use this to consolidate duplicate or fragmented collections.

Unified route to scan, parse, and optionally execute batch imports of directories.

If `dry_run=True`, it only scans the provided paths, attempts to parse creator/set names using the `parsing_template`, and returns a preview of what would be imported. If `dry_run=False`, it launches the actual import process as an asynchronous background task.

schema.title: Response Bulk Update Sets Api Sets Bulk Update Post
Response Bulk Delete Sets Api Sets Bulk Delete Post

post.description:
Resynchronize a set with its local filesystem directory.

Scans the `local_path` associated with the set. Any new image files found in the directory that aren't already in the database will be imported and added to the set.

schema.title: Response Read Settings Api Settings  Get

get.description: Get a specific setting by key.

put.description:
Update or create an application configuration setting.

If the setting key already exists, its value and description are updated. If it does not exist, a new configuration key is created. This allows dynamic reconfiguration of paths and app behavior.

post.description:
Apply a bulk update to multiple images simultaneously.

This endpoint takes a list of image IDs and a data payload, applying the changes to all specified images in a single transaction. List-like fields (e.g. tags) can be appended or overwritten based on the operation_mode.

schema.title: Response Bulk Update Images Api Images Bulk Update Post

post.description: Move multiple images to a different set.

schema.title: Response Bulk Move Images Api Images Bulk Move Post

get.description:
Retrieve a paginated list of all images in the vault.

Supports comprehensive filtering via `search` (matching filename, tags, or notes) and `rating`. Returns images enriched with context like their parent set title and associated creators.
Get all groups of duplicate images.

schema.title: Response Read Duplicate Groups Api Images Duplicates Groups Get

post.description:
Resolve a group of visually identical duplicate images.

This endpoint retains the specified `keep_image_id` and permanently deletes the files and database records for all `remove_image_ids`. Use this carefully as deletion is irreversible.

schema.title: Response Resolve Duplicates Api Images Duplicates Resolve Post

get.summary: Read Random Image File Path Tags
get.description: Get a random image file based on ratio and tags in the path (DisplayFusion compatible).
Get a random image file based on ratio in the path (DisplayFusion compatible).
Get a random image based on filters.
Get a random image file based on filters.

Retrieve aggregated dashboard statistics and system health alerts.

Provides high-level library stats (total images, sets, creators, disk usage) alongside actionable health alerts (e.g., missing thumbnails, orphaned files) that require user attention.

post.description:
Start a background library audit to detect filesystem and database inconsistencies.

The audit scans for 'ghost' records (database entries missing files) and 'orphan' files (files missing database records). It clears any previously pending issues before starting a fresh scan.

schema.title: Response Start Audit Api Audit Start Post

get.description: Find the currently running audit task if any.

schema.title: Response Get Current Audit Api Audit Current Get

post.description:
Execute bulk resolution actions for discovered audit issues.

Supports various fix strategies like 'purge' (delete DB record), 'repair' (fix paths), 'delete_file' (remove from disk), 'import' (add to existing set), and 'create_and_import' (create new set and import).

schema.title: Response Resolve Audit Issues Api Audit Resolve Post

get.description:
Retrieve a list of unique tags matching the search query.

Aggregates tags from both Images and Sets, returning a deduplicated, alphabetically sorted list. Useful for building autocomplete dropdowns or tag clouds in the UI.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Prefix or keyword to search for in tags
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Prefix or keyword to search for in tags

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of tags to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of tags to return

schema.title: Response Search Tags Api Tags  Get

get.description:
Serve a cached JPEG thumbnail for the requested image ID.

If the thumbnail does not exist in the on-disk cache (`db/thumbs/`), it is generated on-the-fly from the original high-resolution image using OpenCV and then served. Future requests will serve the cached file directly.

issue_ids.title: Issue Ids
issue_ids.description: List of issue IDs to apply the fix to.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: ID of the audit task that discovered this issue.

issue_type.title: Issue Type
issue_type.description: Type of issue: 'ghost' (DB record with no file) or 'orphan' (file with no DB record).

path.title: Path
path.description: The file path associated with the issue.

directory.title: Directory
directory.description: The parent directory of the file, useful for grouping.

image_id.title: Image Id
image_id.description: The DB image ID if applicable (e.g., for ghost records).

set_id.title: Set Id
set_id.description: The DB set ID if applicable.

expected_phash.title: Expected Phash
expected_phash.description: The expected perceptual hash if known.

found_phash.title: Found Phash
found_phash.description: The actual perceptual hash calculated during the scan.

match_issue_id.title: Match Issue Id
match_issue_id.description: ID of a related issue if they are paired (e.g., a moved file).

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique identifier for the audit issue.

set_title.title: Set Title
set_title.description: Title of the associated set for UI display.

creator_name.title: Creator Name
creator_name.description: Name of the associated creator for UI display.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of issues matching the query.

deep_scan.title: Deep Scan
deep_scan.description: If true, recalculates file hashes to verify integrity rather than just checking existence.

source_path.title: Source Path
source_path.description: Path of the item being imported.

creator_name.title: Creator Name
creator_name.description: Resolved creator name for this item.

set_title.title: Set Title
set_title.description: Resolved set title for this item.

error.title: Error
error.description: Error message if the import failed.

isValid.title: Isvalid
isValid.description: Indicates if the item passed pre-import validation.

scan_auto_path.title: Scan Auto Path
scan_auto_path.description: If true, automatically scans the configured auto-import directory.

dry_run.title: Dry Run
dry_run.description: If true, only returns what would happen without actually importing.

parsing_template.title: Parsing Template
parsing_template.description: Regex template used to extract Creator and Set names from the directory structure (e.g., '[Creator] - [Set]').

delete_source_default.title: Delete Source Default
delete_source_default.description: Default value for delete_source applied to scanned items.

summary.title: Summary
summary.description: Summary statistics of the import operation.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Background task ID if the import was queued asynchronously.
